title: Basic SVO word order
rule: The neutral Chinese sentence pattern is Subject + Verb + Object. Time and place normally come before the verb phrase.
tip: Build simple sentences first, then add time, place, or manner before the verb.

title: 是 for noun identity
rule: Use 是 to say that one noun is another noun. Do not use 是 directly before ordinary adjectives.
tip: For adjective predicates, Chinese often uses 很 even when English does not say very.

title: Adjectives with 很
rule: Adjectives can act like verbs in Chinese. 很 often links the subject and adjective and may be neutral in meaning.
tip: Use 太...了 when you truly mean too or very: 太好了！

title: Questions with 吗
rule: Add 吗 to the end of a statement to make a yes/no question.
tip: Keep normal statement order; 吗 does the question work.

title: 的 for possession
rule: Use 的 after a possessor or modifier before a noun.
tip: 的 is often dropped with close relationships: 我妈妈, 我家.

title: Measure words
rule: Numbers and demonstratives normally need a measure word before a noun.
tip: 个 is the default measure word, but common nouns have their own: 本书, 杯茶, 张纸.

title: 了 for completed actions
rule: 了 after a verb can mark a completed action, especially when the action has a clear object, amount, or result.

examples.py: Wǒ mǎi le yì běn shū.

mistakes: Wrong: 了 only means past tense.
Correct: 了 marks completion or change, not tense by itself.
tip: Past time words can appear without 了 when the sentence is descriptive or habitual.

title: Sentence-final 了
rule: Sentence-final 了 signals a new situation or a change of state.
mistakes: Wrong: 我昨天忙了 for a simple past description.
tip: Ask whether something became true. If yes, final 了 may fit.

title: 在 for location and ongoing action
rule: 在 can mark location before a place, or ongoing action before a verb.
tip: Location 在 + place comes before the main verb.

title: 会, 能, 可以
rule: 会 is learned ability or likelihood, 能 is physical/conditional ability, 可以 is permission or acceptability.
mistakes: Wrong: 我可以游泳 if you mean learned skill.
tip: For permission questions, 可以 is usually the safest: 我可以进来吗？

title: Past negation with 没
rule: Use 没 or 没有 to negate completed past actions and 有.

examples.en: I did not go to school yesterday.

mistakes: Wrong: 我昨天不去学校 for a completed past event.
tip: 不 negates intention or habit; 没 negates occurrence/completion.

title: 因为...所以...
rule: 因为 gives the reason and 所以 gives the result. Chinese often keeps both connectors.

examples.py: Yīnwèi xià yǔ, suǒyǐ wǒ méi qù.
examples.en: Because it rained, I did not go.

mistakes: In English avoid because...so..., but Chinese can use 因为...所以....
Do not put 所以 before the reason.
tip: In casual speech, one connector may be omitted when context is clear.

title: 虽然...但是...
rule: 虽然 introduces a concession and 但是 introduces the contrast.

examples.cn: 虽然很难，但是我会坚持。
examples.py: Suīrán hěn nán, dànshì wǒ huì jiānchí.
examples.en: Although it is difficult, I will persist.

mistakes: Wrong: 虽然很难，我会坚持。
Better: 虽然很难，但是我会坚持。
tip: 但是 can be shortened to 但 in speech and writing.

title: 一边...一边...
rule: Use 一边...一边... for two actions happening at the same time.

examples.cn: 她一边听音乐一边写作业。
examples.py: Tā yìbiān tīng yīnyuè yìbiān xiě zuòyè.
examples.en: She listens to music while doing homework.

mistakes: Use it for simultaneous actions, not sequence.
tip: The subject can appear before the first 一边.

title: 把 sentence basics
rule: 把 moves a definite object before the verb when the sentence focuses on how the object is handled or changed.

examples.py: Qǐng bǎ shū fàng zài zhuōzi shang.
examples.en: Please put the book on the table.

tip: A 把 sentence usually needs a result, direction, location, or complement after the verb.

title: 被 passive
rule: 被 marks the doer in passive sentences, often when something happens to the subject.

examples.py: Wǒ de shǒujī bèi tā ná zǒu le.
examples.en: My phone was taken away by him.

mistakes: 被 is common for unpleasant or affected situations.
Neutral passives often use no 被 at all.
tip: 被 can omit the agent: 手机被拿走了。

title: 越来越
rule: 越来越 + adjective or verb phrase means more and more.

examples.en: Chinese is becoming more and more interesting.

tip: For two linked variables, use 越...越...: 越学越喜欢。

title: 又 and 再
rule: 又 means again for something that has happened; 再 means again for something future or requested.

examples.cn: 他昨天来了，今天又来了。
examples.py: Tā zuótiān lái le, jīntiān yòu lái le.
examples.en: He came yesterday and came again today.

tip: Past again: 又. Future/request again: 再.

title: 不仅...而且...
rule: Use 不仅...而且... to add a stronger second point: not only...but also....

examples.cn: 他不仅聪明，而且很努力。
examples.py: Tā bùjǐn cōngming, érqiě hěn nǔlì.
examples.en: He is not only smart but also hardworking.

mistakes: Wrong: 不仅...但是... for plain addition.
tip: The second clause often carries the more important information.

title: 只要...就...
rule: 只要 gives a sufficient condition; 就 introduces what will happen if that condition is met.

examples.py: Zhǐyào nǐ jiānchí, jiù huì jìnbù.
examples.en: As long as you persist, you will improve.

mistakes: 只要 is not the same as 只有.
只要 = as long as; 只有...才 = only if.
tip: Use 就 to make the condition-result relationship crisp.

title: 只有...才...
rule: 只有 marks a necessary condition; 才 introduces the result that happens only under that condition.

examples.cn: 只有多练习，才会说得流利。
examples.py: Zhǐyǒu duō liànxí, cái huì shuō de liúlì.
examples.en: Only by practicing a lot can you speak fluently.

mistakes: Wrong: 只有...就... for only if.
tip: Compare: 只要努力就会进步; 只有努力才会进步.

title: 无论/不管...都...
rule: 无论 or 不管 means no matter; 都 or 也 marks the unchanged result.

examples.cn: 不管多忙，我都要学习中文。
examples.py: Bùguǎn duō máng, wǒ dōu yào xuéxí Zhōngwén.
examples.en: No matter how busy I am, I will study Chinese.

mistakes: Do not forget 都/也 in the result clause.
无论 is more formal than 不管.
tip: Pattern: 不管 + question phrase/adjective + 都/也 + result.

title: 连...都/也...
rule: 连 highlights an extreme or surprising example; 都/也 emphasizes inclusion.

examples.cn: 这个字连老师都不认识。
examples.py: Zhège zì lián lǎoshī dōu bú rènshi.
examples.en: Even the teacher does not know this character.

tip: The item after 连 should be remarkable in context.

title: 得 complement of degree
rule: 得 after a verb links the action to a degree or evaluation.

examples.py: Tā shuō Zhōngwén shuō de hěn liúlì.

mistakes: For verb-object phrases, repeat the verb before 得.
Remember 的 modifies nouns, 地 modifies verbs, 得 comments after verbs.
tip: 得 complements are useful for describing how well, fast, clearly, or badly an action is done.
